title: To enable the Department of Labor to promote the necessity of artificial intelligence to safeguard the welfare and job security of apprentices.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The AI policy aims to promote the use of artificial intelligence to protect the welfare and job security of apprentices. This policy seeks to enable the Department of Labor to effectively use AI for the benefit of apprentices.

title: To prohibit Artificial Intelligence Data Centers on Federal land.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.
summary: This policy aims to prohibit the development and operation of Artificial Intelligence Data Centers on federal land. Its purpose is to regulate or restrict the use of federal land for AI data centers.

title: To establish the applicability of antitrust laws to the sharing of artificial intelligence frontier model risks, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy aims to clarify whether antitrust laws apply to the sharing of risks associated with artificial intelligence frontier models. It is intended for other purposes as well, but specifics are not available.

title: To amend the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to provide further transparency for the use of content that is substantially generated by artificial intelligence in political advertisements by requiring such advertisements to include a statement within the contents of the advertisements if generative AI was used to generate any image, audio, or video footage in the advertisements, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
summary: This policy document aims to require political advertisements using artificial intelligence-generated content to clearly disclose this use within the ad itself. The goal is to increase transparency and provide voters with more information about the advertisements they view.

title: To provide for Federal oversight of the development and deployment of frontier artificial intelligence in interstate and foreign commerce, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This policy document aims to provide federal oversight of the development and deployment of advanced artificial intelligence in both domestic and international commerce. The policy seeks to achieve this through federal regulation for various purposes.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a policy aimed at safeguarding consumers from misleading or false information presented by artificial intelligence systems. The bill's goal is to prevent deceptive AIs from causing harm to individuals by setting clear guidelines and regulations.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy document that likely aims to promote the use of artificial intelligence by the Small Business Administration (SBA). The specifics of the policy are unclear without further information.

title: To direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to seek to enter into an agreement with a federally funded research and development center for the conduct of an independent evaluation of artificial intelligence systems in use by the Veterans Health Administration, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs.
summary: This bill directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to conduct an independent evaluation of artificial intelligence systems in use by the Veterans Health Administration. The evaluation aims to assess how effectively these AI systems are working and identify potential improvements.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to limit or pause the development and operation of large artificial intelligence data centers. Its exact details and objectives are unknown due to the lack of description available.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to establish guidelines for the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in the workforce. The policy likely focuses on ensuring the AI systems are trustworthy and beneficial to employees and the broader economy.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a proposed policy designed to regulate and provide clarity on the use, development, and sharing of artificial intelligence (AI) models. The specifics of the bill are currently unavailable, but its purpose seems to center around AI models' governance and management.

title: A bill to support an interdisciplinary research community for the advancement of artificial intelligence and AI-powered innovation through partnerships and capacity building at certain institutions of higher education and other institutions, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill aims to support research and innovation in artificial intelligence by strengthening partnerships and capacity building at institutions of higher education and other institutions. It is intended to promote the advancement and development of AI-powered innovation.

title: A bill to exempt AI data centers from bonus depreciation and require data center operators to submit certain information relating to electricity and water use by data centers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to make AI data centers exempt from bonus depreciation incentives for tax purposes. It also requires operators of these data centers to submit information about their electricity and water use.

title: A bill to prohibit the use of certain artificial intelligence models across the Federal Government, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: A proposed law aims to ban the use of specific artificial intelligence models across the US Federal Government. The bill's purpose includes restrictions on certain AI models, but further details are not provided.

title: A bill to establish a National Workforce Transition Board to support training and education activities for workers, in response to the adoption of artificial intelligence and emerging technology, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to create a National Workforce Transition Board to help workers adapt to changes brought about by artificial intelligence and emerging technologies. The board's purpose is to support training and education activities for those affected by these changes.

title: A bill to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a working group to facilitate coordination among relevant stakeholders regarding compound risks relating to artificial intelligence physical systems, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill proposes the creation of a working group by the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to coordinate efforts on AI-related compound risks. The goal is to address the potential dangers associated with physical AI systems in conjunction with other relevant stakeholders.

title: A bill to improve the tracking and processing of security and safety incidents and risks associated with artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill proposes to enhance the monitoring and handling of security and safety concerns linked to artificial intelligence. It also aims to address various other related issues.

title: Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025 aims to protect citizens from unjust or discriminatory treatment by artificial intelligence systems. It likely establishes guidelines and regulations to ensure AI systems are fair, transparent, and respect individuals' rights.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is a proposed law aiming to address the potential environmental effects of artificial intelligence. The specifics of the law are not available, but it likely intends to regulate AI development to mitigate harm to the environment.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 appears to be a law aimed at regulating Artificial Intelligence (AI) usage in various sectors. It is likely an attempt to establish standards, guidelines or oversight for AI implementation in industries covered by the act.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 18 - 15.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to make students in the US K-12 education system knowledgeable about and prepared to work with artificial intelligence. The goal of the act is likely to equip students with skills to effectively utilize AI in various aspects of their lives and education.

title: SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.
summary: The SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026 aims to track and report AI adoption across Small Business Administration programs, requiring the collection of data on the use of AI technologies. This act focuses on monitoring and understanding AI adoption in small businesses to better support and develop effective policies.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This policy is intended to protect families from the rising costs of energy used by AI data centers, but further details are not available. The title suggests the aim is to mitigate the financial impact on families due to AI energy consumption.

title: Conversational AI Services Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Conversational AI Services Act is a policy that likely regulates the use and development of conversational AI systems.

title: Homestake AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: The Homestake AI Act of 2026 appears to be a policy document related to artificial intelligence, but without a description, the specifics of its purpose and scope are unclear. It is likely a legislative proposal or act aimed at regulating or governing AI in some manner.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: This policy, the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026, likely aims to regulate and defend against the misuse of AI systems. It may introduce legislation to safeguard against AI threats and ensure responsible development and use of artificial intelligence technologies.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a proposed policy that aims to establish regulations and guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence in the agriculture sector.

title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act is a proposed law aimed at promoting and guiding the development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States. The act seeks to establish standards and regulations for AI to foster leadership and uniformity in its application across the country.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The "Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025" is a proposed law aiming to prevent unfair business practices involving artificial intelligence. The goal of the act is to protect consumers and workers from price gouging and wage fixing by AI-powered companies.
